content.claim: Students remaining in ordinary primary tracks after selection show deteriorating test scores and reduced performance relative to peers in selective tracks, indicating selection relocates rather than concentrates advantage.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Described by source as based on weaker identification methods than main track-gain results
Causal attribution requires strong assumptions about comparability of track and non-track students
Magnitude of long-run 'loser' effect on non-selected students not precisely quantified in search results
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
